title: hydrocotyle conferta
intro: Hydrocotyle conferta, also known as Pacific water pennywort, is a type of aquatic plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its round leaves and creeping stems that thrive in shallow water environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hydrocotyle conferta include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and possibly lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the extract in supplement form to take advantage of its purported medicinal properties.
